RULING:
PLAINTIFFS JOHN CASEY and PATRICIA CASEY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Argued and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Plaintiffs' motion for summary adjudication as to defendant Hamilton Materials' affirmative defenses is granted as to affirmative defenses numbers 9, 10, 12, 13, 24 and 25 because defendant did not dispute them and is granted as to affirmative defenses numbers 2, 7, 11, 18, 19 and 20 because plaintiffs sustained their burden of production and defendant failed to present evidence creating a triable issue as to any of these affirmative defenses. Defendant Turner Construction Co.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Nov-05-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Steve Healy, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCEL, appearing for plaintiff and opposing party John Casey
Albert Thuesen III of JACKSON JENKINS RENSTROM LLP, appearing for defendant and moving party Turner Construction Company

RULING:
Defendant Turner Construction Co.'s Motion For Summary Judgment
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Turner Construction Co.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Casey was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' request for a continuance is denied. In light of the tentative ruling on the motion for summary judgment, the two discovery motions to be heard concurrently with the summary judgment motion will go off calendar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Lavena Ward CSR# 7077 = (220/HEK)

Off Calendar Nov-02-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Eric Sentlinger, Esq. of DEHAY ELLISTON (510-285-0750), appearing for defendant / moving party Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.
Oren P. Noah, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties John Casey, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Mr. Bauer's deposition testimony and declaration, Mr. Brumley's depositon testimony, Mr. Casey's deposition testimony and declaration, and Mr. Kirk's deposition testimony create triable issues whether Mr. Casey was exposed to asbestos-containing Kaiser Gypsum drywall mud. Defendant's objections to the declarations of Mr. Bauer and plaintiff and the deposition testimony of Mr. Brumley are overruled. The court did not consider the lengthy declaration of Mr. Cohen. The Court further denied defendant's motion for summary adjudication on the ground that defendant's failure to properly address both issues in the separate statement. Order signed in open court. Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

Jeffrey M. Vucinich, Esq. of CLAPP MORONEY BELLAGAMBA VUCINICH BEEMAN SCHELEY (415-874-3562), appearing for defendant / moving party Dynalectric Company
James P. Nevin, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties John Casey, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT DYNALECTRIC COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Dynalectric Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. The declarations of Mr. Gheno and Kenneth Cohen create a triable issue whether Mr. Casey was exposed to asbestos-containing fireproofing attributable to defendant. Defendant's objection to Mr. Gheno's declaration and objection 2 to Kenneth Cohen's declaration are overruled. Defendant failed to satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and are not likely to be able to obtain evidence that defendant acted negligently. The evidentiary materials submitted with the reply papers were not considered. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
